contributions.description: External network exposure cannot be confirmed

evidence.description: Compose port mapping

warnings: CRD502: Runtime permissions cannot be confirmed
CRD503: External network exposure cannot be confirmed

remediations.title: Harden pull_request_target use
remediations.why: pull_request_target runs with base-repository context and can expose privileged resources if untrusted content is executed.
remediations.suggested_action: Avoid checking out or executing pull-request head content in this workflow; separate untrusted validation from privileged operations.

remediations.title: Remove privileged mode
remediations.why: Privileged containers receive broad host-facing capabilities.
remediations.suggested_action: Set privileged to false and grant only the specific capabilities and devices the workload requires.

remediations.title: Remove Docker socket mounts
remediations.why: Access to the Docker socket commonly provides control over the container host.
remediations.suggested_action: Remove the socket mount; if an integration is unavoidable, use a narrowly authorized proxy and isolate the workload.

remediations.title: Reduce GitHub Actions permissions
remediations.why: Write-capable workflow tokens can alter repository or package state.
remediations.suggested_action: Declare only the specific read or write scopes required by the affected job, preferring job-level least privilege.

remediations.title: Avoid passing secrets through shell commands
remediations.why: Shell interpolation can expose credentials through command construction, tracing, process arguments, or error output.
remediations.suggested_action: Pass the secret through the narrowest supported secret input or environment scope and prevent command tracing; do not interpolate it into command text.

remediations.title: Separate CI and runtime credentials
remediations.why: Sharing one identity across build automation and runtime services couples otherwise independent trust boundaries.
remediations.suggested_action: Issue separate least-privilege credentials for CI and runtime services, with independent rotation and revocation.

remediations.title: Avoid host networking
remediations.why: Host networking removes network namespace isolation and changes service exposure semantics.
remediations.suggested_action: Use an isolated Compose network and explicitly publish only required ports.

remediations.title: Separate production and development credentials
remediations.why: Production-like components should not share broadly scoped credentials with lower-trust environments.
remediations.suggested_action: Use environment-specific credentials and approval controls, with production access restricted to the deployment path.

remediations.title: Restrict writable host bind mounts
remediations.why: Writable host binds let the container modify host files within the mounted path.
remediations.suggested_action: Remove the bind mount, make it read-only, or replace it with a narrowly scoped managed volume.

remediations.title: Pin third-party actions to full commit SHAs
remediations.why: Mutable tags can resolve to different code over time.
remediations.suggested_action: Replace the mutable revision with a reviewed full 40-character commit SHA and use dependency automation to track updates.

remediations.title: Restrict credential scope
remediations.why: The credential reaches more workflow or service scope than may be necessary.
remediations.suggested_action: Limit the credential to the smallest job, step, environment, or service scope that requires it and use separate credentials for independent consumers.

remediations.title: Use Compose secrets for sensitive values
remediations.why: Plain environment mappings broaden credential visibility inside a container and its tooling.
remediations.suggested_action: Use a Compose secret or an external secret injection mechanism supported by the deployment environment, scoped to the receiving service.

remediations.title: Review published host ports
remediations.why: Published ports may increase service reachability depending on host firewall and network configuration.
remediations.suggested_action: Remove unnecessary port publishing or bind it to an appropriate interface, then enforce host and network access controls.

remediations.title: Use a verified non-root container user
remediations.why: Static configuration cannot confirm a non-root runtime identity when user is omitted.
remediations.suggested_action: Set and verify a dedicated numeric non-root user in the image and Compose service, with only required filesystem permissions.

remediations.title: Review unresolved reusable workflow
remediations.why: The called workflow was intentionally not fetched, so its behavior and effective secret handling remain unknown.
remediations.suggested_action: Review the referenced workflow at the pinned revision and restrict secrets and permissions passed to it.

remediations.title: Verify runtime security controls
remediations.why: Static repository analysis cannot prove effective runtime permissions or external network exposure.
remediations.suggested_action: Validate the deployed service identity, capabilities, firewall, bind address, and network policy in the target environment.
